what_it_does: This file exports a React component that renders the mobile portrait layout for the surface app: a tall-frame shell with a thin top control bar, a centered wheel stage with a legend, a bottom scrubber, and a bottom-sheet disclosure for the selected item.
observations: Mobile portrait-specific layout component with class layout--portrait
Top chrome is a single thin bar split into bar-left and bar-right control groups
Left controls: LensChip, SpaceChip, LayerChip, ResChip, QuantChip
Right controls: CentreChip, LiveDot, ViewToggle, Settings
DecisionsBar is placed in-flow directly below the header
A portrait-stage groups Legend and a full-center WheelOrState
Disclosure is rendered as variant sheet only when the gallery is not open
A Tether component receives the selected items sequence number
The top bar carries a stamp attribute ui://chrome/topbar
standouts: The header comment explicitly states the design contract: 390x844 portrait, wheel owns the frame, chrome is one thin row, detail is a bottom sheet, with a word budget of 2-4
Use of stamp on the top bar to assign a ui:// address
Disclosure variant sheet and conditional rendering based on galleryOpen
Optical centering of the wheel via a dedicated portrait-stage that includes the legend
conventions: Functional React component with typed props ({ s: SurfaceState })
BEM-like CSS class names: layout--portrait, bar--top, bar-left, bar-right, center--full
Conditional rendering with && for optional components
Props are passed through from SurfaceState without local state
concerns: The top bar packs many chips into a single row and could overflow on narrow portrait widths
Disclosure is rendered when galleryOpen is false but its behavior when point is null is delegated to the child component
Tether receives selected?.seq without a visible fallback if undefined
No explicit handling of landscape or tablet form factors within this file
notes: Portrait.tsx is the tall-frame mobile shell: minimal top chrome, centered wheel stage, bottom scrubber, and an on-demand bottom-sheet detail panel.
questions: What fields does SurfaceState expose beyond those used here?
How does WheelOrState decide between rendering the wheel and a state placeholder?
What visual or functional role does Tether perform?
What is the semantics of stamp and how is ui://chrome/topbar consumed?
How is galleryOpen toggled and why does it suppress the Disclosure?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the header comment matches the implementation: a native portrait layout where the wheel dominates, controls live in one top row, and detail appears as a bottom sheet.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a tall-frame mobile interface that feels native to portrait phones by giving the wheel visual dominance, collapsing controls into a single top row, and revealing detail only when summoned.
novelty: The explicit design contract in the comment (word budget 2-4, wheel owns the frame, not a shrunk desktop) and the optical-centering stage that combines legend and wheel as one vertical group.
contribution: A self-contained portrait layout composition that arranges notice, top chrome, decisions bar, legend, wheel, scrubber, and bottom-sheet disclosure into a tall-frame mobile viewport.
summary_for_embedding: Portrait.tsx is a React layout component that renders a mobile portrait shell with a thin top control bar, a centered wheel stage with legend, a bottom scrubber, and a nonmodal bottom-sheet disclosure for selected detail.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a native tall-frame portrait interface where the wheel dominates the viewport and detail is shown on demand as a bottom sheet.

symbols.does: Renders the mobile portrait layout shell for the surface app.
symbols.description: Portrait is a React functional component that receives SurfaceState and composes a tall-frame viewport. It places a notice, a thin top bar split into left and right control clusters, an in-flow decisions bar, a portrait stage containing a legend and a centered wheel-or-state view, a bottom scrubber, and a nonmodal bottom-sheet disclosure for the selected item, plus a tether tied to the selected sequence.
